title: Japanâ€™s Kaneka Breaks Ground on First Biomass Steam Plant in Malaysia
content: Kaneka and BAC Renewable Energy are building their first biomass steam plant in Gebeng, Malaysia, set to start in Q3 2026. The RM31 million plant will produce green steam to replace natural gas, cutting about 30,000 tons of COâ‚‚ a year. The project involves partners like Knight Frank Project Services, Boilermech and Bank Islam Malaysia, and supports Kaneka Malaysiaâ€™s goal of 70% emission cuts by 2030 and net zero by 2050.
author: Analysts Account
